spec.description: A reference dashboard of common LogQL query patterns. Copy any panel and replace the label names/values with your own to get started quickly.

options.content:
## 📖 LogQL Query Templates

This dashboard is a **reference library** of common LogQL query patterns. Each panel demonstrates a specific query technique.

### How to copy a template
1. Click the panel menu (⋮) → **Edit**
2. Copy the query from the query editor
3. Paste into your own dashboard or Explore
4. Replace placeholders like `your-app-name` and `your-namespace` with your actual label names and values

| Section | What's covered |
|---|---|
| **Basic Queries** | Stream selectors, label matchers |
| **Line Filters** | `\|=` contains, `!=` exclude, `\|~` regex |
| **Parsing** | JSON, logfmt, pattern, regexp extraction |
| **Metrics from Logs** | `rate()`, `count_over_time()`, `bytes_rate()` |
| **Aggregations** | `unwrap`, `quantile_over_time`, `avg_over_time` |

> 💡 **Tip:** Use **Explore** (compass icon in the left sidebar) to test and refine queries before adding them to a dashboard.

spec.title: JSON Parsing + Field Filter
spec.description:
Parse structured JSON logs and filter on extracted fields. Works when each log line is a JSON object.

🔧 Customize:
• | json extracts all top-level keys as labels
• Filter extracted fields: | level="error"
• Access nested fields: | json field="outer.inner"
• Rename on parse: | json status="http_status_code"
• Numeric filter: | response_time > 1000

spec.title: logfmt Parsing + Duration Filter
spec.description:
Parse key=value formatted logs (common in Go services). Filter on numeric fields with comparisons.

🔧 Customize:
• logfmt parses key=value pairs
• Duration fields support units: > 1s, > 500ms, > 2m
• Combine filters: | level="error" | duration > 500ms
• Filter strings: | method="GET"
• Works with Go standard library logger format

spec.title: Pattern Extraction
spec.description:
Extract specific fields by matching a fixed-format log structure. Simpler than regex for structured logs.

🔧 Customize:
• <_> skips a field you don't need
• <label_name> captures into a named label
• Adjust the template to match your log format exactly
• Example log: 2024-01-01 level=error msg="connection refused"
• After extraction: | level="error" to filter

spec.title: Regexp Label Extraction
spec.description:
Use RE2 named capture groups to extract labels from unstructured log lines.

🔧 Customize:
• (?P<name>pattern) creates a label called 'name'
• Capture multiple fields in one expression
• Example: "(?P<level>\\w+)\\s+(?P<status>\\d{3})"
• Filter extracted labels after: | level="error"
• Use for non-standard log formats where pattern/json/logfmt won't work

spec.title: Multi-Stage Pipeline (parse → filter → reformat)
spec.description:
Chain parse → filter → reformat in one pipeline. The most powerful LogQL pattern.

🔧 Customize:
• | json or | logfmt first to extract fields
• Filter with label comparisons and line filters
• | line_format rewrites the displayed line using Go template syntax
• {{.label_name}} references an extracted label
• Great for cleaning up noisy log formats for readability

spec.expr: {app="your-app-name"} | json | level=~"error|warn" | status_code >= 400 | line_format "[{{.level}}] status={{.status_code}} msg={{.message}}"

spec.title: Basic Stream Selection
spec.description:
The foundation of every LogQL query. Select log streams using label matchers inside {}.

🔧 Customize:
• Replace 'app' and 'namespace' with your actual label names
• Replace values with your service name and namespace
• Add more labels to narrow the scope: {app="x", env="prod", region="us-east"}

spec.title: Label Regex Matching
spec.description:
Use =~ for regex matching on label values. Useful for selecting multiple services at once.

🔧 Customize:
• =~ matches a regex pattern (RE2 syntax)
• !~ excludes matching label values
• Example: app=~"api-.*" matches any app starting with 'api-'
• Combine: {app=~"svc-a|svc-b", env="prod"}

spec.title: Line Filter — Contains String
spec.description:
Filter log lines to only those containing a specific string. Case-sensitive.

🔧 Customize:
• Replace 'search term' with the string you want to find
• Chain multiple filters: |= "error" |= "database"
• For case-insensitive: use regex instead → |~ "(?i)search term"
• Opposite (exclude): != "unwanted string"

spec.title: Line Filter — Exclude + Regex
spec.description:
Combine exclusion filters and regex to find what you want. Filters chain left-to-right.

🔧 Customize:
• != excludes lines containing the string
• |~ "error|warn|fatal" matches any of the alternatives
• !~ excludes regex matches
• Chain: |= "error" != "expected error" != "test error"

spec.title: Log Volume Rate (lines/sec by app)
spec.description:
Log lines per second, grouped by app. The fundamental volume metric from logs.

🔧 Customize:
• Replace 'your-namespace' with your namespace label value
• Change 'app' in sum by() to group by a different label
• $__auto adjusts the range to match the dashboard time range
• Add filters before [$__auto]: |= "error" to count only error lines
• Unit: set to 'logs/s' or 'short'

spec.title: Error Log Rate (by app)
spec.description:
Rate of error-level log lines, broken down by service. Parse first to get the level label.

🔧 Customize:
• Replace json with logfmt if your logs use key=value format
• Adjust level=~"error|warn" to match your severity values
• Change the sum by(app) label to group differently
• Compare against total rate to get error percentage
• Unit: set to 'logs/s' or 'short'

spec.title: Error Ratio (errors ÷ total)
spec.description:
Fraction of log lines that are errors. Query A = errors, B = total, C = ratio.

🔧 Customize:
• Set panel unit to 'percentunit' (displays 0–1 as 0–100%)
• Match the filter in query A to your error definition
• Query C is a Math expression: $A / $B
• Add thresholds: warn at 0.01 (1%), critical at 0.05 (5%)
• Use stat panel type for a single current-value KPI

spec.title: Top 5 Noisiest Apps (log volume)
spec.description:
Find the top N noisiest services in a namespace by log volume.

🔧 Customize:
• Change 5 to any N (top 3, top 10, etc.)
• Replace 'your-namespace' with your namespace
• Change 'app' in by(app) to group by a different label
• Add a line filter to limit to specific log types
• Great for identifying unexpectedly chatty services

spec.title: count_over_time by Log Level
spec.description:
Count of log lines per time bucket, grouped by an extracted label. Use for trend analysis over a time range.

🔧 Customize:
• sum by(level) groups the count by severity level
• Change 'level' to any extracted label (status_code, method, region)
• Unlike rate(), returns raw counts per interval (not per-second)
• $__auto adapts bucket size to the selected time range
• Useful for building log event histograms

spec.title: unwrap — Avg Duration from Logs
spec.description:
Extract a numeric value from parsed logs and compute the average over each time bucket.

🔧 Customize:
• | logfmt or | json to parse the numeric field first
• | unwrap <field_name> extracts the numeric label
• Supports duration suffixes automatically (e.g. 1.5s, 200ms)
• Also works with: sum_over_time(), min_over_time(), max_over_time(), last_over_time()
• Set unit to 'seconds' or 'milliseconds' to match your values

spec.title: quantile_over_time — p95 Latency from Logs
spec.description:
Calculate percentile latency from a numeric field embedded in logs.

🔧 Customize:
• Change 0.95 to 0.5 (p50), 0.99 (p99), etc.
• Replace 'response_time_ms' with your extracted numeric field name
• Use | json or | logfmt before unwrap to extract the field
• by(app) groups percentiles per service — change or remove label
• Set unit to 'milliseconds' or 'seconds'

spec.title: bytes_rate — Log Ingestion Volume per Service
spec.description:
Measures how many bytes of log data are ingested per second, per service.

🔧 Customize:
• bytes_rate() counts raw bytes of log lines (no parsing needed)
• Change 'namespace' label and value to match your environment
• group by a different label: by(cluster), by(pod), etc.
• Set unit to 'Bps' (bytes per second)
• Useful for estimating log ingestion cost per service

spec.title: unwrap — Min / Avg / Max of Numeric Field
spec.description:
Aggregate a numeric field extracted from logs into a table showing min, avg, max, and total per service.

🔧 Customize:
• Each query (A–D) uses a different aggregation: min, avg, max, sum
• Match the parsing stage (json/logfmt) and field name to your logs
• Change 'app' in by() to group by a different dimension
• Switch panel type to 'table' and use 'Last' reducer for a stat summary
• Great for request size, response time, or queue depth analysis

spec.title: LogQL Query Templates
